title: Consensus vs Policy
description: Consensus rules determine whether a block or transaction is valid for the whole network, while policy rules determine what an individual node will relay, mine, or keep in its mempool. A transaction can be consensus-valid yet still be non-standard or locally rejected by policy.

resources.title: Bitcoin Core Academy: Hardcoded Consensus Values
resources.notes: Clear contrast between immutable consensus validation and implementation-specific rule boundaries.

resources.title: Bitcoin Core Academy: Mempool Lifecycle
resources.notes: Shows where local relay and admission policy diverge from raw consensus validity.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Transactions
resources.notes: Practical transaction relay context for understanding standardness and local node policy.
